tagline: Submit and manage distributed training workloads effortlessly.
what_it_does: The AI Runtime CLI (`air`) allows users to submit and manage distributed training workloads on the AI Runtime serverless GPU compute platform directly from their local machines. It utilizes YAML-based job configurations for easy source control integration and supports both workspace-based and git-based workflows. This CLI is particularly useful for long-running or multi-node distributed training jobs that require persistence beyond interactive sessions.
why_it_matters: This update enhances productivity by enabling data scientists and machine learning engineers to manage GPU training workloads without the need for a notebook interface, streamlining the workflow and reducing the time to deployment. It also facilitates better version control and collaboration through YAML configurations, which can be easily integrated into existing CI/CD pipelines.

capabilities.title: YAML Job Configuration
capabilities.desc: Define training jobs declaratively in YAML for easy version control.

capabilities.title: Integration with MLflow
capabilities.desc: Track and manage training runs seamlessly using MLflow.

capabilities.title: Multi-Node Support
capabilities.desc: Run long-running and distributed training jobs across multiple nodes.

capabilities.title: Local Submission
capabilities.desc: Submit GPU workloads directly from your laptop without opening a notebook.

use_cases: Submitting GPU training jobs from local development environments.
Managing long-running training tasks that require persistence.
Collaborating on training job configurations using source control.

steps.desc: Follow the installation guide to set up the AI Runtime CLI on your machine.

steps.title: Configure YAML Jobs
steps.desc: Create YAML configuration files for your training jobs.

steps.title: Submit Workloads
steps.desc: Use the `air` command to submit your training jobs.

steps.title: Track Training Runs
steps.desc: Monitor and manage your training runs through MLflow.

steps.title: Use Custom Docker Images
steps.desc: Integrate custom Docker images for your training environments.

key_takeaway: The AI Runtime CLI empowers users to efficiently manage distributed GPU training workloads directly from their local environments, enhancing productivity and collaboration.
